title: Regime-expert ensemble with segment-wise calibration
summary: F1 pit timing is regime-dependent: slick vs wet, first stint vs later stints, and early vs late race behave differently.
rationale: F1 pit timing is regime-dependent: slick vs wet, first stint vs later stints, and early vs late race behave differently. Calibration literature suggests post-hoc calibration and gated ensembles can improve probability quality under distribution shift even when base models are strong.
implementation_hint: Train separate experts for major regimes such as slick/wet, stint 1 vs 2+, and early/mid/late race, then stack out-of-fold logits with a simple meta-learner or rule-based gate. Apply beta or isotonic calibration per regime using OOF predictions only, then blend calibrated probabilities.
expected_effect: Can reduce probability distortion from mixing incompatible subproblems and may improve public-score robustness if train/test regime mix shifts.
risk: Too many experts can fragment data and overfit; calibration does not guarantee AUC gains unless regime segmentation meaningfully changes ranking.
